infobox.Guest stars:: Max Gail Arden Myrin Jerry Wasserman Jake Busey

sections.heading: Intro
sections.text: "Santabarbaratown 2" is the first episode of Season Seven , following on directly from the Season Six finale " Santabarbaratown ". As Henry is struggling for his life in a Santa Barbara hospital, Shawn is determined to catch his father's shooter at all costs.

sections.heading: Contents
sections.text: 1 Plot Summary 2 Trivia 3 Gallery 4 Promo 5 Digital Releases

sections.heading: Plot Summary
sections.text:
Shawn has just realized his father’s life is in danger. He rushes to find him, but it’s too late. One of Henry 's old partners, Jerry Carp , has already shot Henry because Henry found out Jerry was involved in a police scandal years ago, and Jerry needed to keep Henry quiet. Shawn rushes Henry to the hospital. After Henry makes it out of surgery okay, Juliet urges Shawn to not get involved with catching Henry’s shooter. Shawn doesn’t listen; he recruits Gus and they set out to find and arrest Carp.
Shawn and Gus head to Carp’s beach house to investigate. There, Shawn sees that Carp’s home is far too nice for a retired cop. He determines that Carp is involved in a much larger scandal, and someone is helping him flee the country to avoid arrest. Just then, Lassiter and Juliet show up and are annoyed that Shawn and Gus got there first. In Carp’s hidden basement, they find a storage filled with automatic weapons and ammo. Shawn realizes most of this loot came from a place called “Red Chief Firing Range”.
Shawn and Gus head to the firing range to investigate. Shawn asks to see the owner, but the employees say the owner, Jack Atwater, doesn’t come out unless there’s trouble. Right then, Shawn remembers that Jack Atwater was also one of Henry’s old partners — the guy the SBPD put behind bars just last week. Shawn and Gus go to visit Atwater, who agrees to help any way he can after hearing that Henry was shot. However, on his way to the police transport, he is shot by a long-range sniper and killed before they can get any answers.
Back at the SBPD, the gang searches for leads on Atwater’s shooter. Chief Vick has a private conversation with Shawn, where she more or less lets him know, without actually telling him, to go after Carp. Now, armed with the Chief’s approval to keep investigating, Shawn goes undercover as “Soupcan Sam” and heads back to the firing range to get a look at their “Hall of Fame” list — a list depicting the best shooters that ever shot a gun at Red Chief. Shawn believes that whoever took that perfect long-range shot at Atwater must be on this list. He notices one man’s name, a J.E. Vest, and realizes he saw this guy in a photo with Carp. Vest must have been involved in the scandal. He could also be the shooter.
Shawn and Gus head back to the hospital to ask Henry about J.E. Vest. Henry tells him (in writing since he can't speak yet) that Vest now works private security at a non-profit organization called “Feed Everyone”. Shawn realizes that the owner of the organization, Julian Drake, is at a bookstore doing a signing and figures Vest is probably there working security for him.
At the bookstore, Shawn and Gus see Vest and immediately approach him. They end up causing a scene, leading to Vest and his fellow security officer to take control of the situation and throw the guys out of the bookstore. Vest steals one of Shawn’s business cards, and now he knows who he is and where he and Gus work. At this point, Gus wants to back out, but an eager Shawn convinces him to keep going.
Back at the Psych office , Shawn and Gus sit down to discuss their next move. But before they can get a word out, their lives are threatened by a landmine that has been planted under their couch — a special landmine that will trigger when they stand up. Shawn and Gus realize that Carp had a cache of these same landmines in the weapons closet at his beach house. They manage to call the SBPD to come in and freeze the device before it blows. The SBPD are able to disarm the bomb and Shawn and Gus are saved.
Shawn and Gus’ next move is to find Vest, so they head to the headquarters of “Feed Everyone.” There, Shawn realizes that “Feed Everyone” is a scam organization and that Drake’s “non-profit” is supplying weapons to Africa, not food. Shawn also realizes that Carp’s been the one supplying Drake’s scam with automatic weapons, which explains why Carp was living in a swanky beach house. Just then, Drake’s other security guard shows up and points a gun at Shawn and Gus. But, it turns out he’s FBI and has been investigating Drake undercover for months.
Back at the SBPD, Chief Vick informs everyone that they have to back off the Carp case because the FBI now has jurisdiction. But, Shawn knows he needs to catch Carp himself — for his father. Shawn follows a lead he saw in the FBI officer’s case file to a house where Carp, Drake, and the security detail have set up.
At the house, through binoculars, Shawn sees Carp there and decides that he and Gus need to break in immediately and bring Carp down. Against Gus’ wishes, Shawn tries to break in, but the house is fortified and the situation proves to be too dangerous. They also nearly destroy The Blueberry in the process.
Later, Shawn and Gus are surprised to learn that Lassiter is determined to stay on the case despite the Chief’s order to stay off of it. Lassiter knows they are too close to finding Carp to stop now. So, Shawn and Lassiter devise a plan that involves sending an order of Chinese food laced with a vomit-inducing agent to the house where Carp is at. Later that night, at the house where Carp is at, Shawn and Lassiter see one of the guards start to vomit; they now know their plan is working. With the guards now distracted by their sickness, Shawn kills the power in the house and makes his way inside.
He searches throughout the house and finds Carp. Shawn is finally face to face with his father’s shooter. Shawn pulls a gun on Carp and starts to lead him out of the house for arrest, but Shawn is stopped when Julian Drake shows up with a gun pointed at Shawn’s back. Shawn is able to kick Julian off of him, but in the mess of the action, Carp manages to pick up a gun and point it at an unarmed Shawn. Just before Carp is about to pull the trigger, Juliet shows up and takes him down, saving Shawn’s life. She's also able to take down Vest with a shot to the shoulder, bringing the case to a close.
Back at the hospital, the doctor tells Shawn that Henry is on the road to recovery and is going to be just fine.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: It is revealed that Lucinda Barry apparently died at some point in the series, as there's a plaque on the wall at the Red Chief shooting range indicating that Lassiter won first place at the Lucinda Barry Memorial Tournament. The wall also features another first-place Lassiter plaque as well as a second-place Henry plaque. There's a small pineapple trinket on Shawn's desk in the Psych office . While Henry is being transported to the operating theater, Shawn desperately claims that being shot "isn't serious. This isn't like having appendicitis." This is a reference to Shawn's recent hospitalization for appendicitis in S6E13 " Let's Doo-Wop It Again ". Shawn tells the FBI agent "I don't give a damn about your soft, peachy, Anthony Michael Hall-ish face!" Anthony Michael Hall would later go on to appear as Harris Trout in several episodes of Psych . The fake beard that Shawn and Gus share while sneaking into the shooting range first appeared in S4E16 " Mr. Yin Presents... " and would also feature in Psych: The Movie . Gus eats a can of peaches in the warehouse in a nod to Dulé Hill 's role as Sam in the 2003 film Holes . Shawn introduces Gus as Gern Blanston, which is a reference to a Steve Martin comedy routine where he claims that's his real name. At the shooting range Shawn yells "Wolverines!" in a nod to the 1984 action film Red Dawn . James Earl Vest was probably named after James Earl Ray , who was convicted of the 1968 Martin Luther King Jr. assassination . There are several comical references to the 1987 action flick Lethal Weapon , which is fitting since Shawn, enraged and seeking retribution for his father's shooting, recklessly channels the antics of Mel Gibson's Martin Riggs throughout.
